meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Solve 'No Project Ideas' With Email Automation
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to build automated email SaaS tools using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate markets and generate revenue.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is a symptom of overthinking. The cure is building high-utility email automation tools for specific niches. By using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can ship a revenue-generating MVP in days, not months, while solving genuine business pain points.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat—the universal agony of the 'no project idea' paralysis. You want to build, but you're trapped in the purgatory of aimless ideation, fearing that your next project will be another digital ghost town. The graveyard of SaaS is filled with products that were built, but never needed.

But here is the secret: you don't need a revolutionary idea; you need a boring, profitable problem. Email automation is the backbone of modern business, yet it remains fragmented and overpriced. By building a micro-SaaS that automates email workflows, you aren't just writing code—you are building a bridge between a business problem and its revenue-generating solution.

steps.title: Define the Niche Workflow
steps.description: Identify a specific vertical, like real estate or freelance consulting, that struggles with manual email follow-ups. Focus on one 'high-friction' task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for an email automation SaaS focused on [Niche]. Include a database schema for user profiles and email templates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Tailwind and Supabase for an email automation tool.'

steps.title: Integrate Email Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect to an SMTP provider or API like Resend to handle delivery. This is the core engine of your automation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular service class for sending emails using the Resend API, including error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement Resend API service in /lib/email.ts with robust error handling.'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Create the trigger-action logic that allows users to define when an email should be sent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a background worker function that checks a database of scheduled emails and dispatches them based on user-defined triggers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cron job handler to process queued emails in the database.'

steps.title: Implement User-Facing Dashboard
steps.description: Build the interface where users manage their automation rules. Keep it lean; focus on utility over UI flair.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard UI for users to create, edit, and delete email automation rules. Use a clean, professional aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create the AutomationDashboard component with CRUD operations for email rules.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your MVP to production and get it in front of 5 potential users for feedback immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a deployment checklist and configuration for shipping this project to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure production environment variables and generate a deployment summary.'

pro_tips: Always build for 'unsexy' B2B niches; they pay faster than B2C.
Don't build a custom email editor; integrate a library like TipTap to save 40 hours of work.
Focus on 'Time-to-First-Email'—if a user can't send a test email in 2 minutes, your UX is broken.
Use Supabase Edge Functions for your automation triggers to keep infrastructure costs near zero.
Collect emails even before the product is done; a landing page with a waitlist is your first validation.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, reach out to your identified niche on LinkedIn or Twitter. Don't sell the 'tool'; sell the 'time saved'. Iterate based on the first five users' feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to stop ideating and start executing. The market doesn't care about your perfect plan; it cares about the problem you solve today. Stop waiting for inspiration and start building your asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
